meta_title: For Founders: API integration guide to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: API Integration Guide to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to master API integration to overcome a lack of portfolio. A founder-focused resource using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP fast.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the perfect portfolio to land clients. By integrating high-value APIs like Stripe or OpenAI, you create functional, revenue-generating assets that prove your technical competence. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative deployment to build a production-ready MVP that commands authority and demonstrates real-world SaaS engineering skills.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a founder's greatest enemy, but the 'no portfolio' trap is the silent killer of your first sale. You feel the weight of expectation—clients want proof, but you want to build. You are stuck in a loop of building toy apps that never see the light of production, while your competitors are closing deals with functional, API-driven solutions.

It is time to pivot from 'coding for practice' to 'coding for impact.' By mastering API integrations, you transform your technical ability into a tangible business asset. This guide strips away the academic fluff, focusing on the mechanical precision required to build, integrate, and deploy SaaS features that move the revenue needle.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-Generating Feature
steps.description: Don't build a to-do list. Build a feature that solves a paid problem, like a Stripe-powered subscription gate or an OpenAI content generator.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project architecture for a SaaS MVP that integrates Stripe for payments and OpenAI for text summarization. Focus on a lean, scalable directory structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README and folder structure for a Stripe/OpenAI integration MVP'

steps.title: Bootstrap the API Layer
steps.description: Establish a robust service layer that handles authentication and rate limiting before writing business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API service layer in TypeScript that handles secure key management, error handling, and request retries for a third-party REST API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup an API client module with proper error handling and environment variable management'

steps.title: Implement the Core Integration
steps.description: Connect your backend to the external service, ensuring data flows correctly from the request to the response.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a controller function that takes user input, sends it to the OpenAI API with specific system prompts, and handles the JSON response.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the OpenAI chat completion endpoint with input validation'

steps.title: Automate Data Persistence
steps.description: Ensure every API interaction is logged or saved, creating the 'data trail' that proves your app is working in production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema using Prisma that logs API request metadata, response payloads, and user IDs for auditability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and run a Prisma migration for the API logs table'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Launch the MVP on a live URL. A live link is worth a thousand lines of local code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment configuration file for Vercel or Railway, including environment variable injection for production keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure deployment settings and verify environment secret synchronization'

pro_tips: Always build with 'circuit breakers'—if an API goes down, ensure your UI fails gracefully rather than crashing the entire site.
Use mock servers for development to avoid hitting API rate limits and burning through your developer credits early.
Document your API integration as a 'Case Study' in your portfolio; explain the business problem you solved, not just the code you wrote.
Prioritize webhooks over polling; it makes your app look 10x more professional and reduces operational costs.
Version your API calls within your code; when the third-party provider updates, you won't break your production environment.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is live, build a simple landing page that explains the value proposition of your tool. Start collecting emails or signups to validate market demand immediately.
content.conversion_block: You have the blueprint and the tools to turn your technical curiosity into a profitable SaaS engine. Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
